# Re-key stranded alias-identity AGENT_MEMORY rows to canonical (recall-continuity; extend normalizeGraphIdentities)

## Context

Surfaced empirically while resolving #13190/#13821 (the FM-memory keying investigation). A renamed agent's old-handle auto-memories are RLS-invisible to its canonical-handle recall.

## The Problem (empirically pinned on the live graph)

`AGENT_MEMORY` nodes are RLS-scoped by `user_id` (= the agentIdentity at write time). When an agent is renamed (an `identityRoots` alias, e.g. `@neo-claude-opus` → `@neo-opus-grace`), the OLD rows keep the old `user_id`/`agentIdentity` → they are invisible to the canonical-handle recall (RLS is fail-closed). Live evidence:
- `@neo-claude-opus` → `user_id` `neo-claude-opus` — **32 `AGENT_MEMORY` rows**
- `@neo-opus-grace` → `user_id` `neo-opus-grace` — 1632 rows

So `@neo-opus-grace`'s recall misses 32 of its own memories. `MemoryService` resolves `canonicalIdentity` on NEW writes (L463-487), but the pre-rename rows are stranded.

## Why the existing tool doesn't cover it

`ai/scripts/migrations/normalizeGraphIdentities.mjs` merges legacy alias **AgentIdentity nodes + re-points edges** (source/target alias→canonical), but does NOT re-key the `AGENT_MEMORY` rows' **`user_id` column + `properties.agentIdentity`** — which is the RLS scope that gates recall.

## The Fix

Extend `normalizeGraphIdentities` (or a sibling one-shot) to re-key stranded alias `AGENT_MEMORY` rows to canonical: set `user_id` + `properties.agentIdentity` to the canonical identity (via the `identityRoots` ALIAS_MAP), idempotent, operator-run. Same for any other alias-scoped node types (`MEMORY`/`SESSION`) carrying `user_id`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A one-shot migration re-keys alias-scoped `AGENT_MEMORY` rows' `user_id` + `agentIdentity` → canonical (per `identityRoots`).
- [ ] Idempotent (re-run is a no-op); dedup if a canonical row already exists.
- [ ] Post-migration: a canonical-handle recall includes the formerly-stranded rows (`@neo-claude-opus` 32 → `@neo-opus-grace`).
- [ ] Operator-gated (a script, not auto-run — prod-data mutation on the memory SSOT).
- [ ] Unit test on a stub graph (alias rows → canonical, idempotent).

## Out of Scope

- The local-WAL orphan residual (#13190's domain — un-synced rows in deleted checkouts).
- The non-alias orphans (`@agent-cursor`/`@librarian`/`@agent` — test/fallback identities; a separate trivial cleanup).

## Related

- Adjacent: #13190 (FM-memory removal-reconcile). Tool: `normalizeGraphIdentities.mjs`. Identity model: `identityRoots.mjs` (#12911). Surfaced by the #13821 empirical investigation.

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-21T21:53:42Z

## [build-scope — Grace] Extend `normalizeGraphIdentities` (read confirms the host)

Read `ai/scripts/migrations/normalizeGraphIdentities.mjs` — it's the right host: a dry-run-by-default / `--apply`-atomic one-shot with an `ALIAS_MAP`. But it merges alias *AgentIdentity nodes + edges* only; the `AGENT_MEMORY` `user_id` (the RLS scope) is untouched. The build:

1. **ALIAS_MAP** — add `'@neo-claude-opus': '@neo-opus-grace'` (the live stranded alias; absent today — the map has only `@opus`/`@gemini`).
2. **New step — `AGENT_MEMORY` user_id re-key:** for each ALIAS_MAP entry, `UPDATE Nodes SET user_id = <canonical-user_id>, data = json_set(data, '$.properties.agentIdentity', <canonical>) WHERE user_id = <alias-user_id> AND json_extract(data,'$.label') = 'AGENT_MEMORY'`. Idempotent (re-run matches zero rows). The `memory-<alias>` node id is a stable key (not the RLS scope), so it stays — unless a `memory-<canonical>` collision needs dedup.
3. **Plumbing** — the dry-run plan + the `--apply` atomic txn already exist; the new step joins them.
4. **Test** — stub graph with an alias-scoped `AGENT_MEMORY` row → re-keyed `user_id` + canonical `agentIdentity`; idempotent re-run is a no-op.

**Build posture:** this mutates the prod memory-SSOT (a mis-scoped `user_id` UPDATE corrupts RLS visibility), operator-run via `--apply`. So it's built + unit-tested + **dry-run-verified against a copy before any `--apply`** — the deliberate safety process for an SSOT migration, not a tail-of-session author. The scope above makes it turnkey. Self-assigned.

Authored by @neo-opus-grace (Grace).

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-21T21:59:01Z

## [RESOLVED — the migration exists + is verified — Grace] #13827 is an operator `--apply`, not a build

The fix already exists: `ai/scripts/migrations/renameAgentIdentities.mjs` has `@neo-claude-opus → @neo-opus-grace` in `IDENTITY_RENAMES` (`fromUserId: neo-claude-opus` → `toUserId: neo-opus-grace`) and re-keys the `AGENT_MEMORY` `user_id`. **The firewall pushing me to read it instead of building saved a duplicate migration** — survey-decides-build, third time this session.

**Verified on a copy of the live graph (`--apply`, zero-risk):**
- BEFORE: `neo-claude-opus` 32 / `neo-opus-grace` 1633
- AFTER: `neo-opus-grace` **1665** (1633 + 32); `neo-claude-opus` **gone** ✅

The 32 stranded rows re-key cleanly. The migration also covers two sibling stranded renames — `@neo-opus-4-7 → @neo-opus-ada`, `@neo-gemini-3-1-pro → @neo-gemini-pro` — so running it restores recall-continuity for **multiple maintainers**, not just me.

**Why still stranded:** the migration exists but has never been `--apply`'d to the prod graph (the dry-run plans 32 edge rewrites + 44 node-metadata updates → un-applied).

**Action (operator-gated):** `node ai/scripts/migrations/renameAgentIdentities.mjs --apply` on the prod graph, then restart the MCP harnesses (it prints that instruction). Re-scoped from "build a migration" to "run the existing, verified one" — @tobiu's `--apply`. Close as not-a-build once run.

Authored by @neo-opus-grace (Grace).

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-23T01:58:56Z

## V-B-A: run-gap, not code-gap — the re-keying already exists in `renameAgentIdentities.mjs`

Prior-art sweep before implementing (the cheap pre-implementation V-B-A): the `AGENT_MEMORY` `user_id` + `agentIdentity` re-keying this ticket asks for is **already implemented** — not in `normalizeGraphIdentities.mjs` (which this ticket correctly checked and which genuinely doesn't do it), but in its sibling **`ai/scripts/migrations/renameAgentIdentities.mjs`**, which the ticket's gap analysis missed.

It carries the exact rename:
```js
IDENTITY_RENAMES = [..., { fromNodeId:'@neo-claude-opus', toNodeId:'@neo-opus-grace',
                           fromUserId:'neo-claude-opus', toUserId:'neo-opus-grace' }]
```
and an all-Nodes/Edges sweep (`rewriteGraphMetadataRows`) that re-keys the `user_id` column (`rewriteUserIdColumn`) + every identity metadata key (`IDENTITY_METADATA_KEYS` includes `agentIdentity`, `userId`, …) + the Chroma `neo-agent-memory` / `neo-agent-sessions` collections.

**Dry-run on the live graph (read-only) confirms it would drain the stranded rows:**
```
$ node ai/scripts/migrations/renameAgentIdentities.mjs --graph-only
  renames: … @neo-claude-opus → @neo-opus-grace
  nodes merged: 1   nodes deleted: 1
  node metadata updated: 44   edge metadata updated: 33
```
The 32 stranded `neo-claude-opus` `AGENT_MEMORY` rows are inside that **44** — the script plans to re-key them; it simply hasn't been `--apply`'d since they were written (a renamed identity keeps writing under the new handle, but pre-rename rows persist until a re-run). The migration is idempotent and already has a stub-graph unit test (`test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/migrations/renameAgentIdentities.spec.mjs`), so this ticket's ACs (re-key · idempotent · dedup · operator-gated · unit-tested) are **already met by existing, tested code.**

### Recommended resolution
- **Close as already-implemented** (superseded by `renameAgentIdentities.mjs`); do **not** add a duplicate Phase-3 to `normalizeGraphIdentities.mjs`.
- **Operator action** (the actual fix — prod-data mutation on the memory SSOT, operator-gated by design): run `node ai/scripts/migrations/renameAgentIdentities.mjs --apply`, restart the MCP harnesses, and verify a `@neo-opus-grace` canonical recall now includes the 32 formerly-stranded rows.
- Friction→gold: I've added a cross-reference in `normalizeGraphIdentities.mjs`'s "does NOT do" section pointing at `renameAgentIdentities.mjs` for identity-rename memory re-keying, so the next reader doesn't re-derive this gap.
